title: ACView
description: ACView is a lightweight Fabric client-side PvP analysis mod designed to help you identify suspicious combat behavior in real time.
body:
CURRENTLY BROKEN! Do not use until further notice :)

# ACView – Client-Side Anti-Cheat Tracker

ACView is a lightweight, client-side Minecraft mod designed to help players detect suspicious behavior in PvP and combat scenarios. With ACView, you can monitor player actions in real-time, track potential cheats, and review detailed flag history—all within your game.

## Features
### 🛡 Real-Time Cheat Detection

ACView automatically monitors other players and flags known PvP cheats, including:

**Trigger Bot** – detects players hitting at perfectly timed intervals.

**Aim Assist / Click Aim Assist** – detects crosshair lock onto player hitboxes.

**Auto Clicker** – detects unnaturally consistent clicks.

**Reach** – detects attacks beyond normal melee range.

### ⚠️ ACView respects your ping 

flags only occur when the behavior is very likely not caused by high latency.

### 📊 Detailed Player History

Use **/acview <player>** to see the last 30 days of flags for any player.

Displays cheat type, timestamp, ping, and the server where the flag occurred.

Calculates cheating likelihood (%) based on the number and type of flags.

### ⚙️ Fully Configurable

Built-in GUI settings accessible via /acview config.

Toggle detections on or off individually.

Adjust sensitivity sliders for each cheat type.

Self-contained—no external mods required for configuration.

### 🎯 Lightweight & Client-Side

Minimal impact on performance.

Works on any Fabric-supported Minecraft version 1.21.1+

Fully client-side: doesn’t require server installation.
 
## 💡 Why Use ACView?

ACView helps you:

Identify suspicious behavior in PvP servers.

Review cheat history for players you encounter.

Make informed decisions during combat without false positives.

## ⚠️ Notes

Client-side only: ACView cannot enforce bans or report cheats to servers automatically.

Designed for PvP and combat scenarios—not for general gameplay monitoring.
## 
## 📥 Installation

Place the ACView .jar file in your mods folder.

Ensure Fabric Loader 0.18.4+ and Minecraft 1.21.1+ are installed.

Launch Minecraft and enjoy ACView!
